title: Asterisk Queue Log Abandon Rate and Service Level Breach
app: Asterisk `queue_log` file monitoring via Universal Forwarder
spl:
index=voip sourcetype=asterisk:queue_log
| eval q=coalesce(queue, qname)
| eval is_abandon=if(event="ABANDON",1,0), is_enter=if(event="ENTERQUEUE",1,0), is_answer=if(event="CONNECT",1,0)
| bin _time span=1h
| stats sum(is_enter) as enters, sum(is_abandon) as abandons, sum(is_answer) as answers by _time, host, q
| eval abandon_rate=round(100*abandons/nullif(enters,0),2)
| eval sla_met=round(100*answers/nullif(enters,0),2)
| where enters>30 AND (abandon_rate>15 OR sla_met<75)
description: The Asterisk `queue_log` is the canonical source for ENTERQUEUE, ABANDON, and CONNECT events. Hourly abandon percentage and connect ratio highlight understaffed intervals or misrouted IVR paths before executive dashboards show revenue impact.
value: Protects revenue and NPS on carrier or enterprise contact centers running Asterisk queues by exposing abandon spikes within an hour.
implementation: Monitor `/var/log/asterisk/queue_log` with correct line breaking (each line is one event). Extract `event`, `qname`/`queue`, and hold time fields if present on ABANDON. Tune thresholds: retail often tolerates higher abandons than emergency services. Join with workforce management schedules via lookup on `q`.
visualization: Line chart (abandon_rate, sla_met), Bar chart (worst queues), Heatmap (hour × queue).
knownFalsePositives: Abandon rate noise during IVR or queue redesigns, short staffing, or campaign callbacks where the contact center WFM plan already called for a higher threshold.

references.title: Splunk Docs: Monitor files and directories

detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• `props.conf` `LINE_BREAKER` for queue_log pipe format.
• Clock sync between Asterisk and Splunk.

Step 1 — Validate event counts against `queue show stats`.

Step 2 — Exclude marketing test campaigns via ANI lookup.

Step 3 — Alert routing to WFM team with queue name.

Step 4 — Archive raw queue_log for regulatory retention if required.
grandmaExplanation: We watch queue wait and abandon on call centers. We help you catch understaffing before service level tanks.
telcoUseCase: Contact center queue analytics on Asterisk `queue_log` — correlate abandons with staffing without exporting CDR to a separate warehouse.
